compiled_state.title: As a fix-bug workflow, I can estimate blast radius and annotate escalation dialogs with dependency context
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Gate 2b (blast radius) as a MODIFIER to fix-bug's post-investigation workflow. Estimates blast radius using file location conventions (detected during onboarding) plus fan-in count from static import analysis. Reports results as plain-language annotations in escalation dialog messages.
**Why**: Blast radius provides valuable context for user decisions without independently triggering escalation. When another gate has already flagged a fix, knowing "this file is imported by 12 other modules" helps the user assess risk proportionally.
**Scope**:
- IN: Convention-based heuristics for package manifests, CI/CD config, schema/migration files, entry points; fan-in analysis via ast-grep (when available) or grep-based import counting (fallback); plain-language annotation format; MODIFIER ONLY — never adds a primary signal
- OUT: Centrality-Aware Test Gate (operates at commit time on staged files — no shared logic); Gate 2a/2c/2d (separate stories)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, blast radius is estimated using file location conventions and fan-in analysis, reported as a plain-language annotation in escalation dialog messages
  ← Satisfies: "Blast radius is estimated using two inputs...reported as a plain-language annotation"
- When this story is complete, blast radius never adds a primary escalation signal — it only enriches dialog text when another gate has already triggered
  ← Satisfies: "Blast radius never adds a primary escalation signal"
- When this story is complete, fan-in analysis falls back to grep-based import counting when ast-grep is not installed
  ← Satisfies: "fan-in count from static import analysis (via ast-grep when available, falling back to grep-based import counting)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Grep-based fan-in fallback must produce usable results, not noise — validate against known high-fan-in and low-fan-in files
- [Maintainability] SC5 operates within fix-bug on proposed changes before commit; Centrality-Aware Test Gate operates at commit time — document this boundary

comments.body: All 4 child tasks completed: blast radius script, RED tests, and Gate 2b SKILL.md integration.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
